title: carex conferta var. conferta
intro: Carex conferta var. conferta, also known as coastal sedge, is a type of grass-like perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its flat, rounded leaves and brown seed heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Coastal sedge is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
